title: Forlais Compression Notation Public Papers

required_before_publication: Confirm every PDF has Status Public Release.
Confirm every PDF has Classification Public.
Confirm every PDF has Disclosure status Approved for public release.
Confirm every PDF has Release channel GitHub + Zenodo.
Confirm every PDF has Publication target GitHub + Zenodo public archive.
Confirm metadata/SHA256SUMS.txt matches the final PDFs.

papers.title: The Compression Theorem for Trained Neural Network Weights
papers.reason: The theorem sets the source-coding boundary for every single-source BF16 result in the pack.

papers.title: BF16 1.52x Entropy Ceiling
papers.reason: Core release result. Establishes the 1.52x single-model BF16 ceiling.

papers.title: Benford's Law as the Origin of the BF16 Compression Ceiling
papers.reason: Explains the mantissa entropy mechanism behind the ceiling and gives the closed-form origin.

papers.title: The Mutual-Information Paradox in BF16 Weight Streams
papers.reason: Core mutual-information result. Resolves the apparent 2.4-bit value-space MI into a small and mostly unexploitable signal.

papers.title: Three-Component Decomposition of BF16 Weight Values
papers.reason: Shows sign exponent and mantissa as the near-independent decomposition used by the ceiling papers.

papers.title: The r=2 Law of Mantissa-Bit Bias in Trained Neural Weights
papers.reason: Provides the mantissa-bias law that supports the Benford-derived ceiling.

papers.title: A Closed-Form r=2 Prior for Mantissa Bits and Why Seeding an Adaptive Coder With It Yields No Practical Gain
papers.reason: Safe negative result. Shows the correct prior does not materially improve adaptive coding.

papers.title: A Wide Comparison of Public Lossless Compressors on BF16 Model Weights
papers.reason: Public-compressor envelope demonstrating that mature public configurations cluster below the ceiling.

papers.title: Aligned Byte Compression of BF16 Weights
papers.reason: Simple public-safe transform baseline with a modest verified ratio.

excluded_categories: Fixed-Width Dictionary Coding for BF16 Weights
Synthesis papers that depend on unreleased adjacent entries
